text.title: rag-implementation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rag-implementation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Fixed-size chunking splits mid-sentence, producing semantically incomplete chunks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An incomplete chunk embeds the partial thought, not the full concept. Retrieval scores are lower and the retrieved text is harder for the LLM to use.

failure-mode.description: Using pypdf to load scanned PDFs or complex multi-column layouts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: pypdf text extraction fails on scanned images (needs OCR) and produces garbled text on complex layouts. Use a dedicated OCR pipeline or AWS Textract for such sources.

failure-mode.description: Re-ingesting updated documents without deleting stale chunks first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a delete+re-insert strategy, old chunk versions accumulate. The LLM may retrieve outdated information and produce contradictory answers.

failure-mode.description: Using HyDE (hypothetical document embedding) without accounting for 2x embedding cost per query.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: HyDE generates a hypothetical answer then embeds it. This doubles embedding API cost and adds LLM latency on every query. Budget it explicitly.
